executive_summary: Latest updates from 4 sources covering the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Monitoring 4 key developments. AI analysis refreshing shortly.
execSummaryRich: Escalation: HIGH. Key developments: IRGC says Dimona, Tel Aviv and Eilat targeted in new wave of missiles. Iran military adviser says war continues until compensation, sanctions lifted. ‘We will soon get the Strait of Hormuz open’: Trump urges China, UK and other na. Middle East War Day 14: US aircraft crashes in Iraq, Netanyahu warns of toppling. Situation remains active.

key_developments.headline: IRGC says Dimona, Tel Aviv and Eilat targeted in new wave of missiles
key_developments.detail: Iran’s Revolutionary Guard said it launched a new wave of strikes targeting Israel and US positions, including sites in Dimona, Tel Aviv and Eilat, according to a statement reported by Press TV. “We negotiate with enemies with impact-driven strikes,” the IRGC said, describing the latest phase as hav
key_developments.fullAnalysis: Iran’s Revolutionary Guard said it launched a new wave of strikes targeting Israel and US positions, including sites in Dimona, Tel Aviv and Eilat, according to a statement reported by Press TV. “We negotiate with enemies with impact-driven strikes,” the IRGC said, describing the latest phase as hav

key_developments.headline: Iran military adviser says war continues until compensation, sanctions lifted
key_developments.detail: A senior military adviser to Iran’s leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, said the war will continue until Tehran receives full compensation and sanctions are lifted. Mohsen Rezaei said Iran would keep fighting until “all economic sanctions are lifted, and legally binding international guarantees are obtained”
key_developments.fullAnalysis: A senior military adviser to Iran’s leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, said the war will continue until Tehran receives full compensation and sanctions are lifted. Mohsen Rezaei said Iran would keep fighting until “all economic sanctions are lifted, and legally binding international guarantees are obtained”

key_developments.headline: ‘We will soon get the Strait of Hormuz open’: Trump urges China, UK and other na
key_developments.detail: US President Donald Trump has called on the UK and other countries to send warships to help keep the Strait of Hormuz open as tensions with Iran continue. In a post on his Truth Social platform, Trump urged countries affected by the disruption of shipping in the narrow waterway to deploy naval vesse

key_developments.headline: Middle East War Day 14: US aircraft crashes in Iraq, Netanyahu warns of toppling
key_developments.detail: March 13 marks the fourteenth day of war between the United States-Israel and Iran. The war began on February 28 with joint US and Israeli strikes on Iranian targets. Around 2,000 people have been killed during the strikes on Iran by the US and Israel. Here are the top developments as the war enters

terminology_explained.simple_explanation: Iran's elite Revolutionary Guard — controls missiles and drones.
Narrow waterway — 20% of world oil passes through it.

what_to_watch_next: Monitor live feeds for breaking developments.

india_impact.headline: India Monitors Gulf Conflict
india_impact.detail: India's energy security tied to Gulf stability.
india_impact.full_detail: India imports 85% of crude oil from Gulf. Strait of Hormuz disruption would immediately impact supply.

indiaSummary: India continues to monitor the US-Israel-Iran conflict closely given its significant dependence on Gulf energy imports and the large Indian diaspora. Oil price volatility directly impacts petrol prices for Indian consumers.
